message:
fix(conformal): NaN coverage snaps to 0.95 default instead of silently narrowing intervals (ft-x50ce)

[MEDIUM] `MetricForecaster::new` stored `coverage: coverage.clamp(0.01,
0.999)`. Same `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` gotcha as ft-761tz, ft-b4l62,
ft-jz40p, ft-0p5q5: a NaN coverage survived the clamp and propagated
into `CalibrationSet::quantile` where `NaN * (n+1) → NaN.ceil() → NaN
as usize == 0` → `max(1) == 1` → returns `sorted[0]` (the smallest
score).

Downstream `forecast_all` then built `(point - q, point + q)` intervals
using the smallest possible q — producing the NARROWEST interval the
calibration data can support instead of the configured coverage.
That silently undercuts the formal-coverage guarantee that is the
entire point of conformal prediction: alerting thresholds driven by
these intervals over-fire and the `coverage: NaN` surfaced in
`ResourceForecast` looks broken but still ships.

## Fix (two layers)

1. **`MetricForecaster::new`** — snap NaN coverage to the default
   0.95 (matching `ConformalConfig::default()` at line 76) BEFORE the
   clamp runs. Callers whose config deserialised NaN now fall back
   to a sensible coverage rather than silently-undercut intervals.
2. **`CalibrationSet::quantile`** — defense-in-depth: return `None`
   on NaN coverage so external callers reaching `quantile` directly
   (bypassing the forecaster boundary) fail closed.

## Tests (2 new)

* `ft_x50ce_predictor_rejects_nan_coverage_at_construction` — pins
  that `MetricForecaster::new(.., f64::NAN)` stores 0.95.
* `ft_x50ce_quantile_nan_coverage_returns_none` — pins the inner
  defense so a direct `CalibrationSet::quantile(NaN)` is None, not
  the silently-smallest score.

## Verification

`cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features` on a
fresh `/tmp/ft-rv-p3-target` — lib compiles clean (189 pre-existing
warnings, no new ones).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
